LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS JENNY HE AND CENTURY 21 REALTY ALLIANCE'S (AMENDED) MOTION FOR ORDER DISCHARGING STAKEHOLDER AND FOR RELATED ORDER IS GRANTED. ONCE DEFENDANTS HE AND CENTURY 21 REALTY ALLIANCE DEPOSIT $5,000 WITH THE COURT, THEY WILL BE DISCHARGED FROM LIABILITY. AFTER THE DEPOSIT, THEY CAN RECOVER $1,500 AS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES PER CCP 386.6. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STANLEY SHEN'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED BECAUSE (1) PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE A PROOF OF SERVICE SHOWING THAT ALL NAMED DEFENDANTS WERE SERVED WITH THIS MOTION; (2) THE ACTION IS STAYED; (3) THE MOTION IS UNNECESSARY BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT THE DISPUTED FUNDS HAVE BEEN INTERPLEADED WITH THE COURT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Ntc Of Petition; Petition To Enforce Arbitration Clause And List Of Exhibits
Off Calendar Jun-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - STANLEY SHEN OF THE SHEN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 661-6700 PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
TONY FU, TEL. NO. 867-5973, CROSS-COMPLAINANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. DAMON M. THURSTON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF RANKIN, SPROAT & REYNOLDS, TEL. NO. (510) 465-3922 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT;

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT STANLEY SHEN'S Petition To Enforce Arbitration Clause is Granted. Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendant entered into an "Employment Agreement" that provides for the arbitration of any "dispute arising under this Agreement or in connection with Attorney's services hereunder." (Declaration of Damon M. Thurston, Ex. A, Complaint, Ex. 1.) The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") arises out of Cross-Defendant's representation of Cross-Complainant. Although Cross-Complainant argues that he was not Cross-Defendant's client, the FACC contradicts this argument. (FACC, par. 40.) No evidence is presented in support of Cross-Complainant's argument that he was fraudulently induced to enter into the Employment Agreement. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Discharging Stakeholder And For Related Order
Off Calendar Jul-25-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs WEN TUNG and KEVIN CHIN's Motion For Order Discharging Stakeholder And For Related Order is granted. Once defendants Tung and Kevin Chin deposit $15,000 with the Court, they will be discharged from liability. After the deposit, they can recover $1,500 as reasonable attorney's fees per CCP 386.6. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion For Pltfs' Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jul-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: andres sanchez, esq., 415.512.9865, for defendants jian cai mao and jing ci mao; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STANLEY SHEN's Motion For Preliminary Injunction is denied because (1) plaintiff fails to provide a proof of service showing that all named defendants were served with this motion; (2) the action is stayed; (3) the motion is unnecessary because it appears that the disputed funds have been interpleaded with the Court. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
